---
title: "Resources"
---

A curated collection of tools, tutorials, datasets, and links useful for research in evolutionary theory, mathematical biology, and computational modeling.

---

## Lab Code & Software

All lab software is hosted in the [Pichugin-Lab GitHub organization](https://github.com/Pichugin-Lab). Individual repositories will be linked here as they are released.

| Repository | Description | Language | Tutorial |  Website |
|------------|-------------|----------|----------|----------|
| [P. falciparum microsattelite analysis](https://github.com/Pichugin-Lab/pf_microsattelite) | Utilizing [Anderson et al., 1999](https://doi.org/10.1017/s0031182099004552) | R/Python | **Coming Soon** | |
| [P. falciparum antigen sequencing](https://github.com/Pichugin-Lab/pf_targeted_sequencing) |  | R / Python | **Coming Soon** | |
| [P. falciparum Nextstrain](https://github.com/Pichugin-Lab/chminextstrain) | pfcsp and pfama1 are currently supported | Python | **Coming Soon** | [csp-full](https://nextstrain.org/groups/PichuginLab-Private/sydney/chmi/csp-full) (Login Required) |

---

# External Resources

- [MALGEN7](https://apps.malariagen.net/apps/pf7/) 

- [NOMADS Community · GitHub](https://github.com/nomads-community)
	- [Nomadic](https://jasonahendry.github.io/nomadic/)
	- [GitHub - JasonAHendry/multiply: Multiplex PCR design, in silico · GitHub](https://github.com/JasonAHendry/multiply)
	- Bioinformatic trainings [GitHub - nomads-community/bioinformatics: Bioinformatic 
	- Resources · GitHub](https://github.com/nomads-community/bioinformatics)
	- [broadinstitute/malaria - illumina amplicon sequencing pipeline](https://github.com/broadinstitute/malaria)
		- Illumina Amplicon Workspace
	- [mad4hatter - nanopore amplicon sequencing pipeline](https://github.com/EPPIcenter/mad4hatter)
		- Publication: [Sensitive and modular amplicon sequencing of Plasmodium falciparum diversity and resistance for research and public health \| Scientific Reports](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-025-94716-5)
		- Protocol:  [Cost-effective targeted nanopore sequencing of P. falciparum malaria V.2](https://www.protocols.io/view/cost-effective-targeted-nanopore-sequencing-of-p-f-yxmvm25m6g3p/v2)

- TERRA 
	- [Q&A: How Terra became a backbone of public health pathogen surveillance \| Broad Institute](https://www.broadinstitute.org/news/qa-how-terra-became-backbone-public-health-pathogen-surveillance)

## Protocols

**Coming Soon**

## Publications 

**Coming Soon**

## Recommended Reading

**Coming Soon**

### Textbooks

**Coming Soon**

### Review Articles

**Coming Soon**

---

## Online Tools & Databases

- [bioRxiv](https://www.biorxiv.org) — preprint server for the life sciences
- [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com) — academic literature search
- [ORCID](https://orcid.org) — researcher identifiers
- [Zenodo](https://zenodo.org) — open-access data and code repository
- [GitHub](https://github.com/Pichugin-Lab) — Pichugin Lab code repositories

---

## Tutorials & Guides

### Getting Started with Quarto

This website is built with [Quarto](https://quarto.org). Quarto is an open-source publishing system that supports Python, R, Julia, and Observable JS.

- [Quarto documentation](https://quarto.org/docs/guide/)
- [Creating a website with Quarto](https://quarto.org/docs/websites/)

### Scientific Computing

- [NumPy](https://numpy.org/doc/) — numerical computing in Python
- [SciPy](https://docs.scipy.org) — scientific computing in Python
- [Julia documentation](https://docs.julialang.org) — high-performance scientific computing
- [R for Data Science](https://r4ds.hadley.nz) — data analysis and visualization in R

---
